client_context.ai_history: No prior AI/ML initiatives. This engagement is a UI modernization and IAM project; AI-assisted data entry identified as a potential future phase only.

problem_statement.title: IBMi Green Screen Modernization for Healthcare Organization
problem_statement.summary: Regional Health Partners operates 15+ mission-critical clinical and administrative applications on IBM AS/400 (IBMi) accessed via 5250 green screen terminals. The organization needs modern web-based UIs with human-centered design, robust IAM (SSO, MFA, RBAC), mobile access for clinical staff, and HIPAA-compliant architecture — while preserving existing RPG business logic and DB2/400 data integrity.

current_state.process_description: All clinical and administrative workflows (patient registration, scheduling, billing, pharmacy, lab orders, clinical documentation) run on IBM AS/400 via 5250 green screen terminals. Staff must memorize function key sequences and terminal navigation. Training takes weeks. No mobile access. No modern authentication (SSO/MFA). 2 RPG developers maintain 15+ applications with limited documentation.
pain_points: Cannot recruit staff willing to use green screen terminals — losing candidates to competitors with modern UIs
Accessibility non-compliance (ADA/Section 508) — green screens fail WCAG standards
No mobile access for clinical staff working across 3 facilities
New hire training takes 3-4 weeks just to learn terminal navigation and function keys
No integration with modern EHR systems or health information exchanges
Aging RPG developer workforce (both developers within 5 years of retirement)
HIPAA audit findings on access control — no MFA, no granular RBAC, shared terminal logins
No audit trail for PHI access — compliance risk growing each year
Change management risk — clinical staff resistant to change after 15+ years on same system
current_state.annual_cost: Estimated $800K/year in lost productivity, recruitment failures, compliance risk, and maintenance of legacy RPG expertise
current_state.volume: 500+ daily users across 3 facilities, ~50,000 transactions/day, 2M+ patient records in DB2/400

desired_state.description: Modern responsive web UI layer with human-centered design principles that wraps existing IBMi business logic via API-first architecture. Role-based access control with SSO and MFA. Mobile-friendly interfaces for clinical staff. Full HIPAA compliance with audit trails. Phased rollout with extensive change management and training.
target_improvements: Modern responsive web UI accessible from any device (desktop, tablet, mobile)
Role-based access control (RBAC) with SSO (SAML/OIDC) and MFA
90% reduction in new hire training time (from 3-4 weeks to 2-3 days)
HIPAA-compliant audit trail for all PHI access
Mobile-friendly clinical workflows for nursing and physician staff
API-first architecture enabling future EHR integrations
Gradual transition path preserving IBMi business logic and data

ai_suitability_assessment.rationale: This is primarily a UI modernization and IAM project, not an AI/ML initiative. The core work involves wrapping legacy IBMi applications with modern web UIs and implementing IAM. AI has limited applicability in the current phase — potential future use for AI-assisted data entry, clinical decision support, or natural language search of patient records.
favorable_factors: Large volume of structured data in DB2/400 could benefit from AI-powered search
Repetitive data entry tasks could be AI-assisted in a future phase
Clinical documentation could benefit from NLP/summarization
risk_factors: HIPAA compliance adds significant complexity to any AI implementation
Clinical staff have low technology maturity — AI features may overwhelm during initial modernization
PHI data requires strict guardrails before any AI processing

pain_points: Recruitment: Cannot attract talent willing to work with green screen terminals
Accessibility: ADA/Section 508 non-compliance with 5250 terminal interfaces
Mobility: No mobile access for clinical staff across 3 facilities
Training: 3-4 week onboarding for terminal navigation alone
Integration: No connectivity to modern EHR systems or HIEs
Workforce: RPG developers approaching retirement with limited knowledge transfer
Compliance: HIPAA audit findings on access control and PHI audit trails
Security: Shared terminal logins, no MFA, no granular RBAC
Change management: Clinical staff resistance to technology change

success_criteria.description: New hire training time for system navigation
success_criteria.measurement: Days from first login to independent use, measured via competency assessment

success_criteria.description: Clinical staff using mobile interfaces for daily workflows
success_criteria.measurement: Percentage of clinical staff with 5+ mobile logins per week

success_criteria.description: Zero critical findings in HIPAA access control audit
success_criteria.measurement: Number of critical findings in annual HIPAA audit

success_criteria.description: Zero data loss during migration and parallel operation
success_criteria.measurement: Count of data discrepancies between legacy and modern systems during parallel run
Monthly uptime percentage excluding scheduled maintenance windows

success_criteria.description: Staff satisfaction with new interfaces vs. legacy green screens
success_criteria.measurement: System Usability Scale (SUS) score on 1-10 scale, surveyed quarterly

success_criteria.description: WCAG 2.1 AA conformance across all modernized interfaces
success_criteria.measurement: Percentage of pages passing automated WCAG 2.1 AA audit (axe-core)

stakeholders.name: CIO
stakeholders.communication_preference: Weekly executive summary, monthly steering committee

stakeholders.name: CFO
stakeholders.communication_preference: Monthly cost tracking, quarterly ROI review

stakeholders.name: Director of Nursing
stakeholders.communication_preference: Weekly sprint demos, direct input on UI design

stakeholders.name: Clinical staff
stakeholders.communication_preference: Training sessions, feedback surveys, change champion network

functional_requirements.title: Modern web UI for patient registration
functional_requirements.description: Replace 5250 green screen patient registration with responsive web interface. Must support all existing registration fields and workflows with improved usability.
acceptance_criteria: All current registration fields available in web UI
Form validation with clear error messages
Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile

functional_requirements.title: Modern web UI for scheduling
functional_requirements.description: Replace green screen scheduling with visual calendar interface. Support drag-and-drop, multi-facility view, and provider availability.

functional_requirements.title: Modern web UI for billing
functional_requirements.description: Replace green screen billing with modern interface. Support claim management, payment processing views, and reporting dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: All billing codes and workflows supported

functional_requirements.title: Modern web UI for pharmacy
functional_requirements.description: Replace green screen pharmacy module with modern interface. Support medication management, order verification, and inventory tracking.

functional_requirements.title: Modern web UI for lab orders
functional_requirements.description: Replace green screen lab order system with modern interface. Support order entry, result viewing, and result notification.
acceptance_criteria: Lab order entry with template support
Result viewing with normal range highlighting

functional_requirements.title: Modern web UI for clinical documentation
functional_requirements.description: Replace green screen clinical documentation with modern interface. Support structured note entry, templates, and document management.

functional_requirements.title: Identity and Access Management (IAM)
functional_requirements.description: Implement modern IAM with SSO (SAML 2.0/OIDC), MFA, and RBAC. Replace shared terminal logins with individual user accounts tied to clinical roles.
acceptance_criteria: MFA for all users (TOTP, push notification, or hardware token)
Role-based access control with clinical role mapping
Session management with auto-logout for PHI protection
Audit trail for all authentication events

functional_requirements.title: API gateway for IBMi integration
functional_requirements.description: Build API layer that wraps existing RPG programs and DB2/400 data access. Provides REST APIs consumed by modern web UIs.
acceptance_criteria: REST API endpoints for all green screen functions
Data transformation between EBCDIC/DB2 and modern JSON formats
Connection pooling and caching for IBMi performance
Rate limiting and circuit breaker patterns

functional_requirements.title: Mobile-responsive clinical workflows
functional_requirements.description: Key clinical workflows (patient lookup, vitals entry, medication administration) accessible from tablets and smartphones.
acceptance_criteria: Critical workflows available on smartphone form factor
Offline capability for basic patient lookup

functional_requirements.title: PHI audit trail and compliance logging
functional_requirements.description: Comprehensive audit logging for all PHI access, modifications, and exports. Support HIPAA audit requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Log all PHI access with user, timestamp, record, and action
Audit report generation for HIPAA compliance
Real-time alerting on suspicious access patterns

functional_requirements.title: Change management and training portal
functional_requirements.description: In-app training guides, contextual help, and feedback mechanisms to support clinical staff transition from green screens.

functional_requirements.title: Reporting and analytics dashboard
functional_requirements.description: Executive dashboard for operational metrics, system usage, and KPI tracking. Replaces legacy report generation.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled report generation and email delivery

scalability.projected_growth: 10-15% annual growth in users and transactions

sources.name: DB2/400 Patient Database
sources.quality_notes: Well-structured relational data with 20+ years of history. Some legacy field formats (packed decimal, EBCDIC). Generally high quality due to validation in RPG programs.

sources.name: RPG Program Library
sources.quality_notes: Business logic source code. Limited documentation. Knowledge concentrated in 2 developers.

sources.name: COBOL Batch Jobs
sources.quality_notes: Batch reporting and reconciliation. Must continue running during and after modernization.

technology_restrictions: Must maintain IBMi backend and DB2/400 during and after modernization — no rip-and-replace
PHI data must remain on-premises or in HIPAA-compliant cloud (BAA required)
Must support Internet Explorer 11 for legacy workstations (Phase 1 only)
No open-source databases for PHI storage without security review
constraints.team_constraints: Small internal IT team (5 people, only 2 RPG developers). Will require external consulting team. RPG developers critical for knowledge transfer and API layer design.

in_scope: Modern web UI for top 6 green screen applications (registration, scheduling, billing, pharmacy, lab, clinical docs)
IAM implementation (SSO, MFA, RBAC) with HIPAA-compliant audit logging
API gateway layer wrapping IBMi RPG programs and DB2/400 data
Change management planning and training materials
HIPAA compliance architecture and audit trail
out_of_scope: Replacing IBMi backend or migrating off DB2/400
EHR system selection or implementation (future phase)
Rewriting RPG business logic (wrap, don't rewrite)
scope_boundaries.justification: Scope focuses on maximum user impact (UI modernization + IAM) while minimizing risk by preserving proven IBMi business logic. Out-of-scope items are deferred to future phases to manage budget and timeline constraints.

assumptions: IBMi system has available capacity for API layer connections (connection pooling via IBM i Access ODBC/JDBC)
Client will provide dedicated RPG developer time (minimum 50%) for knowledge transfer and API collaboration
Existing DB2/400 data is structurally sound and does not require major data cleansing
Client will procure and configure identity provider (IdP) for SSO — consulting team provides integration
Network connectivity between IBMi and modern application servers is adequate
Client stakeholders are available for bi-weekly sprint demos and feedback
COBOL batch jobs will continue running unmodified on IBMi during and after modernization
Client will designate change champions in each clinical department for training rollout
Budget covers external consulting team but not client internal staff costs
